meta.title: BMW M21 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: BMW M21 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW M21 is BMW's first production diesel — a 2.4-litre straight-six based on the M20 petrol, prized for its simplicity and character. Backed by an estimated 80+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 80% confidence — based on enthusiast community consensus, specialist workshop feedback, and the engine's well‑documented history as BMW's first diesel [ESTIMATED].

rows.score: 12 (Mix of simple mechanical repairs (injection pump, glow plugs) and more complex ones (cracked heads, cam wear))
8 (Parts are increasingly scarce; many components are specialist‑order or NLA (no longer available))
12 (Reconditioned cost (£2,750) as % of lowest typical host value (~£5,000 E30 324td) = 55% — 45–59% bracket → 12)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: "The M21 is a robust, simple diesel pioneer — but ageing injection pumps and head cracking are risks — buy with documented history and budget for specialist maintenance." (Clause 1: 8 words. Clause 2: 9 words. Clause 3: 7 words. Total: 24 words.)
verdictRating.bestFor: Classic BMW enthusiasts who appreciate the novelty of BMW's first diesel and don't mind hands‑on maintenance.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You need daily‑driver reliability without access to a specialist familiar with 1980s diesel injection systems.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: (Exclusive mode — no cross-link within this ecosystem; Lincoln fitment is historical only)

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M21 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].

cta.label: → Compare BMW M21 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Cost — "How much does a BMW M21 engine replacement cost?"
items.answer: A reconditioned M21 typically costs £2,000–£3,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], with rebuilt examples reaching £5,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,100 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the BMW M21 a reliable engine?"
items.answer: The M21 scores 3★ out of 5 with 80% confidence [ESTIMATED]. It's fundamentally robust with its cast‑iron block and SOHC design, but age and parts scarcity make reliability increasingly dependent on maintenance history and specialist care.

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common BMW M21 failure?"
items.answer: Injection pump wear and timing issues are common, as is head cracking on tuned examples. Oil leaks from ageing seals and gaskets are also frequently reported. Camshaft wear can occur on higher‑mileage units.

items.question: Fitment — "Which BMW models use the M21?"
items.answer: The M21 powered the E30 324d/324td, E28 524d/524td, E34 524td, and E23/E32 7‑Series diesel variants. It was BMW's first production diesel, introduced in 1983 and produced until 1991.

items.question: Longevity — "How long does the BMW M21 last?"
items.answer: With regular maintenance — timely timing belt changes, injection pump servicing, and oil changes — the M21 can exceed 150,000–200,000 miles. However, parts availability is increasingly a limiting factor for very high‑mileage examples.
